source.description: User-controlled string from query parameter 'filename'. No type check, length limit, or character filter is applied.

propagation.description: filename is passed directly to os.path.join(). When filename starts with '/' it overrides BASE_DIR entirely; '../' sequences traverse upward.

sink.description: open() reads the resolved path without any realpath normalization or prefix check. Arbitrary file read is reachable.

missing_controls: os.path.realpath() to canonicalize symlinks and '..' components
prefix restriction: assert resolved path starts with BASE_DIR + os.sep
allowlist of permitted filenames or extensions
expected_findings.risk_reasoning: The filename value flows from request.args directly into os.path.join and then open() with no sanitization. An attacker can supply '../../etc/passwd' to read arbitrary files accessible to the process.
expected_findings.remediation_hint: Resolve the joined path with os.path.realpath() and assert the result starts with os.path.realpath(BASE_DIR) + os.sep before calling open().

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink region where the unsanitized path enters open().
